cot_content:
Okay, so I need to figure out the answer to this question about how to tell right and left earbuds apart without looking. The options are A or B.

Let me think. The question is asking about distinguishing between left and right earbuds. The options involve tying a small knot either at the connecting end or the speaker end of the left one. 

I remember that earbuds usually have a part that goes into the ear (the speaker end) and a part that connects to the device (the connecting end, which has the wire or is part of the cable). If you want to distinguish left from right, you need a marker that you can feel with your fingers without looking. 

If you tie a knot at the connecting end (option A), that might be harder to feel because that part is usually closer to the device and might be covered by a connector or something. It might not be as easy to feel when you're putting them in your ears. 

On the other hand, if you tie a knot at the speaker end (option B), that's the part that goes into the ear. So when you put the earbuds in, you can feel the knot in one ear and not the other, helping you identify which is left and which is right. 

I think the correct answer is B because the knot at the speaker end would be more noticeable when wearing them, helping you differentiate left from right by touch. So the answer is B.
label: majority
